channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iraq's top court throws out challenge to new laws, including one increasing Islamic courts authority
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — Iraq’s top court on Tuesday threw out a legal challenge that had temporarily halted three controversial laws passed last month by the country’s parliament.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Disney has become the latest company to change its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives as pressure from the federal government to eliminate such programs continues.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration appeals Maryland judge's ruling blocking birthright citizenship order
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Tuesday said it's appealing a Maryland federal judge's ruling blocking the president's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for people whose parents are not legally in the country.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as an attacker repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — With a mix of humor and graphic detail, Salman Rushdie calmly told a jury Tuesday about the frenzied moments in August 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with terrible injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina man pleads guilty to supporting ISIS, prosecutors say
item.description: Alexander Justin White, 29, was arrested on Dec. 4 at Raleigh-Durham International Airport while he was preparing to board an international flight.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, NewsNation

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A snowstorm blew into the mid-Atlantic states on Tuesday, causing dozens of accidents on icy roads, prompting school closures and stoking worries about possible power outages. The heaviest snowfall — up to 10 inches (25 centimeters) — was expected in parts of Virginia and West Virginia.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Papa John's debuts bath bomb inspired by its signature garlic sauce
item.description: Papa John’s is giving its fans a new way to enjoy its signature garlic sauce this Valentine’s Day. On Tuesday, the pizza chain announced that they created a limited-edition bath bomb inspired by the famous sauce that comes with every pizza order.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of former President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UPDATE: Fire under control, 7 injured in Sun Metro fire
item.description: FINAL UPDATE- Fire knocked down. Crews checking for hot spots. Total of seven patients assessed -- four transported, three patient refusals. EPFD investigators are on scene.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-49er Dana Stubblefield freed from prison after rape conviction overturned
item.description: A judge has granted the release of former San Francisco 49er Dana Stubblefield after his 2020 rape conviction was vacated by a California appeals court last December.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge adjusts ruling blocking Musk, DOGE from Treasury Department payment systems
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday clarified that a ruling preventing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing the Treasury Department’s payment systems does not extend to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Survivors of child abuse at Illinois youth detention centers file more lawsuits in hopes of change
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Marcus Walker said the sexual abuse he experienced two decades ago as a teenager at an Illinois youth detention facility has haunted his life. After a staff member gave him drugs and sexually abused him, he joined a gang hoping it would protect him. It didn't.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin's top education official facing challengers on both flanks
item.description: Wisconsin’s top education official faces challengers on the right and left as she runs for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most US kids aren't gaining fast enough in reading and math. These schools are different
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pilot walks away unscathed after small plane crashes near Hampton Airfield in New Hampshire
item.description: A pilot walked away uninjured after a small plane they were flying crashed near near Hampton Airfield in North Hampton, New Hampshire, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amish community helps with Tennessee flood relief
item.description: "We've done a little bit of everything," said Miller. "We've put in some footers. We've laid block. We've framed house with help.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, NewsNation

item.title: Punk rock pioneer battling stage 4 cancer and brain tumor; family asks for help
item.description: His family is holding a fundraiser to help pay for a nurse, physical therapy, and "day-to-day vital living expenses.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: China expands influence near wealthy Florida enclave as migrants from communist country flood into US
item.description: Gordon G. Chang discussed China's growing influence in the Caribbean region and the national security threat it could present.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Plane that crashed and killed 10 was part of Alaska’s air travel system, essential to daily life
item.description: The commuter flight that crashed and killed 10 people on Alaska sea ice last week was part of the workhorse network of short-hop flights that people rely on daily.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen And Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hegseth says Fort Bragg is coming back, but with a twist
item.description: On Monday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed a memo that orders the Army to rename Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, this time in honor of a different soldier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in brutal Louisiana hammer attack on college student subject of ICE hold request
item.description: Immigration officials have filed a detained hold request on a man accused of randomly attacking a Louisiana college student last week with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trader Joe's, Smith's limit daily egg purchases due to nationwide shortages
item.description: Trader Joe's is limiting egg sales to one dozen per customer daily -- and it is not the only company doing so.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to shut down Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau is likely the next federal agency on the chopping block as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk continue the DOGE agenda to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
